summary: Fixed the false tenant workspace unavailable screen by making tenant-scoped HTTP requests wait for tenant workspace readiness before forwarding, and clarified 403 copy for real backend denials.

acceptance_results.criterion: Workspace API requests emitted while loading wait for a non-loading workspace state.
acceptance_results.reason: The interceptor now filters out loading workspace states before take(1), and the regression spec proves the handler is not invoked until a ready tenant state arrives.

acceptance_results.criterion: Forwarded workspace API requests include X-Tenant-Key when currentTenant exists.
acceptance_results.reason: The focused spec verifies X-Tenant-Key: nrg and X-Tenant-Id: tenant-nrg on the forwarded request.

acceptance_results.criterion: 403 copy distinguishes backend forbidden/not-found from missing tenant assignment.
acceptance_results.reason: The page now maps forbidden and tenant-not-found reasons to backend denial copy instead of the missing assignment message.

cleanup_notes: Installed frontend dependencies in the isolated worktree to run Angular/Karma verification; dependency manifests were unchanged.
No backend service or database migration changed.
completed_work: Created TMP-065 classified defect issue and work order.
Identified root cause: TenantWorkspaceInterceptor took the initial loading workspace emission and could forward tenant API requests without X-Tenant-Key.
Added readiness filtering before forwarding workspace requests.
Added interceptor regression coverage for loading-to-ready tenant header attachment.
Added page403 copy coverage for backend forbidden and tenant-not-found reasons.
known_gaps: The local shell uses Node v24.15.0 while webspa-admin declares Node ^18.19.0 || ^20.9.0; npm reports EBADENGINE as a warning.
The Angular build still reports pre-existing campaign SCSS budget and selector warnings unrelated to this fix.
operator_message: TMP-065 is complete: assigned tenant workspaces wait for tenant readiness before admin API calls are sent, preventing false /403 workspace-unavailable redirects from headerless startup requests.
risk_notes: The change can delay tenant-scoped API calls until Auth0/workspace resolution completes, matching the existing route guard behavior.
Non-workspace requests still bypass the tenant workspace stream.

validation.output_excerpt: TMP-065 assignment_decision allow; no blocking reasons
ok true; only existing historical/schedulable warnings
